name: Peoples State Bank Of Plainview
description_short: Community-focused FDIC-insured bank operating in Minnesota and Wisconsin with checking, savings, mortgages, and business lending services.
description_long:
Peoples State Bank is a regional community bank serving five locations across Minnesota and Wisconsin: Menomonie, Boyceville, Altura, Plainview, and Elgin. The bank has been operating as an FDIC-insured institution backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, positioning itself as a stable financial institution for personal and business customers.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including free Kasasa checking and savings accounts with rewards, certificates of deposit (CDs), individual retirement accounts (IRAs), health savings accounts (HSAs), and youth savings options. For lending, they provide personal loans, auto loans, home mortgages, home equity loans, USDA rural housing loans, and personal term loans. Their business services include business checking and savings accounts, business term loans, commercial real estate loans, equipment financing, operating lines of credit, and SBA loans. They also specialize in agricultural lending with agricultural real estate loans, equipment and livestock financing, and agricultural lines of credit.

Peoples State Bank distinguishes itself through its Kasasa rewards program, which has paid $3,022,766 in rewards since March 2013, and its focus on community involvement and rural markets (evidenced by USDA rural housing and agricultural lending offerings). The bank provides modern digital banking capabilities including mobile banking with mobile deposit, digital wallet services, bill pay, and a recently updated online banking platform. Their emphasis on accessibility (AudioEye-enabled website) and fraud prevention education demonstrates customer-focused operations.

As a small regional bank, Peoples State Bank is best suited for customers in their service areas seeking personalized banking relationships and community-focused financial services. The limitation of their five-location footprint means customers outside these geographic areas would need to consider larger regional or national banks. Their specialization in agricultural and rural lending indicates strong alignment with rural business customers but less emphasis on urban or specialized financial products. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Free Kasasa Cash Checking with ATM fee refunds
Free Kasasa Saver with linked account rewards
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with competitive rates
Home Mortgage Loans and USDA Rural Housing Loans
Home Equity Loans and Auto Loans
Personal Loans and Personal Term Loans
Agricultural Real Estate Loans and Equipment & Livestock Loans
Digital Wallet and Bill Pay services
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full U.S. Government faith and credit
Free Kasasa checking and savings accounts with documented rewards ($3M+ paid since 2013)
Specialized lending for rural customers including USDA Rural Housing Loans
Comprehensive agricultural lending (equipment, livestock, real estate, lines of credit)
Modern digital banking with mobile deposit, digital wallet, and bill pay
No-fee ATM withdrawal refunds on Kasasa Cash checking account
Wide loan portfolio including mortgages, auto, home equity, SBA, and commercial real estate
cons: Limited geographic footprint (5 branches in Minnesota/Wisconsin only) reduces accessibility
$10,000 minimum deposit required to earn stated CD rates
Early withdrawal penalties on certificates of deposit
Rates and fees subject to change without notice, limiting predictability
No mention of online account opening or digital-only banking options despite online banking platform
best_for: Residents of Menomonie, Boyceville, Altura, Plainview, or Elgin seeking community banking relationships
Agricultural businesses and farmers needing specialized rural lending and equipment financing
Small business owners qualifying for SBA loans or commercial real estate financing
Savers seeking competitive yields through Kasasa rewards and CD products
diagnosis: Peoples State Bank is best for customers in their five-location Minnesota and Wisconsin service area who value community banking relationships and personalized service. The main caveat is geographic limitation—customers outside their physical branch areas would need to rely entirely on digital banking, and those seeking specialized services (complex commercial financing, wealth management) may outgrow the institution.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
